meta.description: Local JSON source — replace with Firebase fetch later. User progress keys: completedIds, lastOpenedId.

questions.title: Debounce
questions.shortDescription: Implement debounce(fn, delay) — run fn only after delay ms of inactivity.
questions.description:
Debouncing delays execution until a period of **inactivity** has passed. Example: search input — wait until the user stops typing before firing the API.

Implement a `debounce` function that accepts a callback `fn` and a `wait` duration in milliseconds. The returned function should preserve `this` and arguments when `fn` is eventually called.

tests.name: returns a function
tests.code: if (typeof debounce(() => {}, 100) !== 'function') throw new Error('debounce must return a function');

tests.name: calls fn after delay (single call)
resets timer on rapid calls

questions.title: Counter
questions.shortDescription: Build state that increments when a function is called.
questions.description:
Implement `createCounter()` that returns an object `{ increment, getValue }`.

- `increment()` adds 1 to an internal counter.
- `getValue()` returns the current count.

Use a closure so the count is private.

tests.name: starts at 0
tests.code:
const c = createCounter();
if (c.getValue() !== 0) throw new Error('Expected 0');

tests.name: increments

questions.title: Flatten
questions.shortDescription: Flatten a nested array by one level.
questions.description:
Implement `flattenOnce(arr)` where `arr` may contain numbers or nested arrays of numbers. Return a new array with one level of nesting removed.

Example: `[1, [2, 3], 4]` → `[1, 2, 3, 4]`

tests.name: mixed
tests.code:
const r = flattenOnce([1, [2, 3], 4]);
if (JSON.stringify(r) !== '[1,2,3,4]') throw new Error('wrong: ' + JSON.stringify(r));
